uncertainty_flags: Both plans have denied — this appeal targets River Spring, but a parallel appeal or complaint to Fidelis Care should also be filed simultaneously
Confirm the exact date River Spring received the enrollment application (November 19) — get written confirmation from River Spring intake
NYS DOH may need to be involved if both plans continue to deny; consider filing a Medicaid Fair Hearing if appeals are unsuccessful
The provider (Metro Home Health Agency) should also be involved — they may have documentation of the authorization and their decision to continue services for safety reasons
denial_reason_identified: River Spring denied the January 1–3, 2025 claims because the member's enrollment effective date was January 4, 2025, creating a 3-day gap between the end of Fidelis Care coverage and the start of River Spring coverage.
appeal_grounds: The coverage gap was caused by River Spring's own enrollment processing timeline, not by any action or inaction of the member — the application was submitted November 19, 2024, giving River Spring over six weeks to establish a January 1 effective date

New York State Medicaid managed care regulations and CMS guidance require continuity of care during plan transitions, particularly for medically necessary services; a 3-day lapse in personal care aide services for an 82-year-old member with dementia and mobility limitations represents a direct safety risk

The home health aide's continuation of services during the gap was medically necessary and consistent with the member's active care plan — discontinuation would have posed documented health and safety risks

River Spring, as the receiving PACE plan, has an obligation under its enrollment agreement and New York State PACE regulations to ensure continuity of care from the point of enrollment application, not merely from the administrative effective date

Equity and good faith: the member completed all enrollment steps on November 19; the 46-day processing delay leading to a January 4 effective date (rather than January 1) was entirely within River Spring's control
recommended_attachments: River Spring enrollment application with submission timestamp (November 19, 2024)
Written confirmation from River Spring of application receipt date
Fidelis Care denial letter for the same service dates (CLM-FC-2024-88821)
Active Fidelis Care prior authorization for personal care aide services (through December 31, 2024)
Physician documentation of member's dementia diagnosis and mobility limitations supporting medical necessity of uninterrupted personal care
Metro Home Health Agency letter explaining decision to continue services during gap for patient safety
appeal_letter:
Date: February 14, 2025

River Spring Health — Appeals Department
99 Park Avenue, New York, NY 10016

RE: Appeal of Claim Denial — Claim No. CLM-2025-009187
Member ID: MEM-31504
Service Dates: January 1–3, 2025
Provider: Metro Home Health Agency
Denied Amount: $486.00

Dear River Spring Health Appeals Department,

We are writing on behalf of Member ID MEM-31504 to formally appeal the denial of Claim No. CLM-2025-009187 for personal care aide services rendered January 1–3, 2025. The claim was denied on the basis that the member's enrollment was not effective until January 4, 2025. We respectfully request that River Spring authorize payment for these three days of service and accept responsibility for the coverage gap created by its own enrollment processing timeline.

BACKGROUND

The member is an 82-year-old Bronx resident with moderate dementia and limited mobility who requires daily personal care assistance. The member was enrolled in Fidelis Care Medicaid Managed Care with an active authorization for personal care aide services (4 hours/day) through December 31, 2024.

The member's family submitted a PACE enrollment application to River Spring Health on November 19, 2024 — more than six weeks before the desired January 1, 2025 start date. River Spring processed the application and established an effective enrollment date of January 4, 2025, resulting in a 3-day gap (January 1–3) during which neither Fidelis Care nor River Spring had active coverage in place.

Fidelis Care has separately denied claims for the same three days, citing plan termination on December 31, 2024. The member is thus left with no coverage for a period of medically necessary, continuous care — despite having done everything required to ensure a seamless transition.

GROUNDS FOR APPEAL

1. The coverage gap was created by River Spring's processing timeline, not the member. The enrollment application was submitted November 19, 2024. River Spring had 43 days to establish a January 1 effective date. The 3-day gap is a direct result of River Spring's administrative processing — the member bears no responsibility for it.

2. New York State Medicaid regulations and CMS PACE rules require continuity of care during enrollment transitions. Regulations governing PACE programs and Medicaid managed care transitions require plans to ensure members do not experience interruption of medically necessary services as a result of administrative transitions. A lapse in personal care services for an 82-year-old member with dementia and mobility impairment constitutes a direct health and safety risk and is inconsistent with these protections.

3. The services were medically necessary and their continuation was clinically appropriate. The member's treating physician has documented that uninterrupted personal care services are medically necessary given the member's dementia diagnosis and mobility limitations. Metro Home Health Agency continued services during the gap period in good faith to protect the member's safety — a decision fully consistent with the member's active care plan.

4. Equity requires that River Spring accept responsibility for the gap. The member and the member's family completed all required steps for enrollment. The 3-day gap exists solely because of the time required to process the application — a process entirely within River Spring's control. It would be inequitable to deny coverage for a gap that the member had no ability to prevent.

REQUEST

We respectfully request that River Spring Health reverse the denial of Claim No. CLM-2025-009187 and authorize payment of $486.00 for personal care aide services rendered January 1–3, 2025.

If River Spring determines it does not have authority to cover this period, we request written confirmation of that determination so that we may escalate this matter to the New York State Department of Health and file a Medicaid Fair Hearing on the member's behalf.

Enclosed documentation is listed in the attachments. Please contact us with any questions.

Sincerely,
[Authorized Representative / Care Coordinator]
Senior Center Services
[Phone] | [Email]
